THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Two — Part Two

The Torah — Second Book

SHEMOT

Names

Chapters 19 through 40

Sinai — The Ten Words — The Covenant Laws — The Golden Calf — The Mishkan

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QExoda (4Q13) · 4QExodb (4Q14) · 4QExodc (4Q15) · 4QpaleoExodm (4Q22)

4QExode (4Q17) · 4QExodf (4Q18) · 4QExodg (4Q19) · 4QExodh (4Q20)

2QExoda (2Q2) · 2QExodb (2Q3) · 2QExodc (2Q4)

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Beta Israel Ancient Canon

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitutions

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

THE SINAI COVENANT — THE TEN WORDS — CHAPTERS 19 THROUGH 24

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 19 The Arrival at Sinai — The Covenant Offer — The Mountain Burns

19:1 On the third new moon after the people of Yisra'EL had gone out of the land of Egypt, on that day they came into the wilderness of Sinai.

19:2 They set out from Refidim and came into the wilderness of Sinai, and they encamped in the wilderness. There Yisra'EL encamped before the mountain.

19:3 While Moshe went up to The Creator, YHWH called to him out of the mountain, saying: Thus you shall say to the house of Yaakov, and tell the people of Yisra'EL:

19:4 You yourselves have seen what I did to the Egyptians, and how I bore you on eagles' wings and brought you to myself.

19:5 Now therefore, if you will indeed obey my voice and keep my covenant, you shall be my treasured possession among all peoples, for all the earth is mine.

19:6 And you shall be to me a kingdom of priests and a holy nation. These are the words that you shall speak to the people of Yisra'EL.

19:7 So Moshe came and called the elders of the people and set before them all these words that YHWH had commanded him.

19:8 All the people answered together and said: All that YHWH has spoken we will do. And Moshe reported the words of the people to YHWH.

19:9 And YHWH said to Moshe: Behold, I am coming to you in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with you, and may also believe you forever. When Moshe told the words of the people to YHWH,

19:10 YHWH said to Moshe: Go to the people and consecrate them today and tomorrow, and let them wash their garments

19:11 And be ready for the third day. For on the third day YHWH will come down on Mount Sinai in the sight of all the people.

19:12 And you shall set limits for the people all around, saying: Take care not to go up into the mountain or touch the edge of it. Whoever touches the mountain shall be put to death.

19:16 On the morning of the third day there were thunders and lightnings and a thick cloud on the mountain and a very loud trumpet blast, so that all the people in the camp trembled.

19:17 Then Moshe brought the people out of the camp to meet The Creator, and they took their stand at the foot of the mountain.

19:18 Now Mount Sinai was wrapped in smoke because YHWH had descended on it in fire. The smoke of it went up like the smoke of a kiln, and the whole mountain trembled greatly.

19:19 And as the sound of the trumpet grew louder and louder, Moshe spoke, and The Creator answered him in thunder.

19:20 YHWH came down on Mount Sinai, to the top of the mountain. And YHWH called Moshe to the top of the mountain, and Moshe went up.

19:24 And YHWH said to him: Go down, and come up bringing Aharon with you. But do not let the priests and the people break through to come up to YHWH, lest he break out against them.

19:25 So Moshe went down to the people and told them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExoda, 4QExodb, and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 19. The Sinai theophany is among the most carefully preserved events in the Qumran Exodus manuscripts. The covenant offer at verses 19:5-6 is attested in the Cave 4 text.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 19 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19:4 — I bore you on eagles' wings and brought you to myself. The entire Exodus is summarized in one image. YHWH did not bring Yisra'EL to a land or a law first — he brought them to himself. Sinai is not primarily legislation. It is a meeting. The covenant is not a legal code imposed from above — it is the terms of a relationship between YHWH and the people he carried through the wilderness on eagles' wings.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 19:5-6 — you shall be my treasured possession among all peoples, for all the earth is mine. And you shall be to me a kingdom of priests and a holy nation. The covenant at Sinai does not make YHWH the exclusive property of Yisra'EL. YHWH declares that all the earth is his before naming Yisra'EL his treasured possession. The covenant is particular — Yisra'EL is chosen — but it operates within a universal frame. YHWH owns all peoples. He chose one among them for a priestly function within the larger whole.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 20 The Ten Words — The Fear of the People

20:1 And The Creator spoke all these words, saying:

20:2 I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.

20:3 You shall have no other deities before me.

20:4 You shall not make for yourself a carved image, or any likeness of anything that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth.

20:5 You shall not bow down to them or serve them, for I YHWH your Creator am a jealous Creator, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children to the third and the fourth generation of those who hate me,

20:6 But showing steadfast love to thousands of those who love me and keep my commandments.

20:7 You shall not take the name of YHWH your Creator in vain, for YHWH will not hold him guiltless who takes his name in vain.

20:8 Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.

20:9 Six days you shall labor, and do all your work,

20:10 But the seventh day is a Sabbath to YHWH your Creator. On it you shall not do any work, you, or your son, or your daughter, your male servant, or your female servant, or your livestock, or the sojourner who is within your gates.

20:11 For in six days YHWH made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, and rested on the seventh day. Therefore YHWH blessed the Sabbath day and made it holy.

20:12 Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long in the land that YHWH your Creator is giving you.

20:13 You shall not murder.

20:14 You shall not commit adultery.

20:15 You shall not steal.

20:16 You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.

20:17 You shall not covet your neighbor's house — you shall not covet your neighbor's wife, or his male servant, or his female servant, or his ox, or his donkey, or anything that is your neighbor's.

20:18 Now when all the people saw the thunder and the flashes of lightning and the sound of the trumpet and the mountain smoking, the people were afraid and trembled, and they stood far off

20:19 And said to Moshe: You speak to us, and we will listen, but do not let The Creator speak to us, lest we die.

20:20 Moshe said to the people: Do not fear, for The Creator has come to test you, that the fear of him may be before you, that you may not sin.

20:21 The people stood far off, while Moshe drew near to the thick darkness where The Creator was.

20:22 And YHWH said to Moshe: Thus you shall say to the people of Yisra'EL: You have seen for yourselves that I have talked with you from heaven.

20:24 An altar of earth you shall make for me and sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your oxen. In every place where I cause my name to be remembered I will come to you and bless you.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExoda, 4QExodb, 4QExodc, and 4QpaleoExodm all preserve portions of Chapter 20 including the Ten Words. The Nash Papyrus — a pre-Qumran Hebrew manuscript dated to approximately the 2nd century BCE — contains the Ten Words and the Shema, making the Ten Words one of the most anciently attested passages in any Hebrew manuscript. The DSS text of the Ten Words is consistent with the Nash Papyrus.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 20 in full including the complete Ten Words.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QpaleoExodm and 4QExodb include an additional commandment between the ninth and tenth words — a command to build an altar on Mount Gerizim. This addition is not in the Masoretic text and is unique to the DSS and to the Samaritan Pentateuch. It is documented here as a DSS variant. The core Ten Words are consistent across all manuscript traditions.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 20:2 — I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery. The Ten Words open not with a command but with a declaration of identity and a recitation of historical action. YHWH identifies himself by what he did. The commandments that follow are not the basis of the relationship — they are the response to a relationship already established by the Exodus. The sequence is critical: redemption precedes law. YHWH brought them out first. He commands them after.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The Ten Words in the DSS are identical in content to the Orit Ge'ez across all ten. The Devarim 5 version of the Ten Words contains minor variations from the Shemot 20 version — the Sabbath rationale in Devarim cites the Exodus while Shemot cites creation. Both rationales are original and complementary — the Sabbath is grounded in both YHWH's creative rest and Yisra'EL's liberation from Egyptian labor. The DSS preserves both versions.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 21 The Book of the Covenant — Laws on Persons

21:1 Now these are the rules that you shall set before them.

21:2 When you buy a Hebrew slave, he shall serve six years, and in the seventh he shall go out free, for nothing.

21:5 But if the slave plainly says: I love my master, my wife, and my children — I will not go out free,

21:6 Then his master shall bring him to The Creator, and he shall bring him to the door or the doorpost. And his master shall bore his ear through with an awl, and he shall be his slave forever.

21:12 Whoever strikes a man so that he dies shall be put to death.

21:13 But if he did not lie in wait for him, but The Creator let him fall into his hand, then I will appoint for you a place to which he may flee.

21:14 But if a man willfully attacks another to kill him by cunning, you shall take him from my altar, that he may die.

21:15 Whoever strikes his father or his mother shall be put to death.

21:16 Whoever steals a man and sells him, and anyone found in possession of him, shall be put to death.

21:17 Whoever curses his father or his mother shall be put to death.

21:22 When men strive together and hit a pregnant woman, so that her children come out, but there is no harm, the one who hit her shall surely be fined, as the woman's husband shall impose on him, and he shall pay as the judges determine.

21:23 But if there is harm, then you shall pay life for life,

21:24 Eye for eye, tooth for tooth, hand for hand, foot for foot,

21:25 Burn for burn, wound for wound, stripe for stripe.

21:28 When an ox gores a man or a woman to death, the ox shall be stoned, and its flesh shall not be eaten, but the owner of the ox shall not be liable.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExoda and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 21. The covenant laws — the Sefer HaBrit, the Book of the Covenant spanning Chapters 21-23 — are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 21 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 21:24 — eye for eye, tooth for tooth. This principle — lex talionis — is the foundational covenant law of proportionality. It is not a license for unlimited revenge. It is a cap on revenge. The punishment must not exceed the injury. In the ancient Near African context this law was radical — it made the life of the poor equal in legal weight to the life of the powerful. An injury to a poor man's eye carried the same legal weight as an injury to a rich man's eye. The covenant law was an equalizer.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 22 The Book of the Covenant — Laws on Property and Social Justice

22:1 If a man steals an ox or a sheep, and kills it or sells it, he shall repay five oxen for an ox, and four sheep for a sheep.

22:2 If a thief is found breaking in and is struck so that he dies, there shall be no bloodguilt for him.

22:7 If a man gives to his neighbor money or goods to keep safe, and it is stolen from the man's house, then, if the thief is found, he shall pay double.

22:14 If a man borrows anything of his neighbor, and it is injured or dies, the owner not being with it, he shall make full restitution.

22:18 You shall not permit a sorceress to live.

22:19 Whoever lies with an animal shall be put to death.

22:20 Whoever sacrifices to any deity, other than YHWH alone, shall be devoted to destruction.

22:21 You shall not wrong a sojourner or oppress him, for you were sojourners in the land of Egypt.

22:22 You shall not mistreat any widow or fatherless child.

22:23 If you do mistreat them, and they cry out to me, I will surely hear their cry,

22:24 And my wrath will burn, and I will kill you with the sword, and your wives shall become widows and your children fatherless.

22:25 If you lend money to any of my people with you who is poor, you shall not be like a moneylender to him, and you shall not exact interest from him.

22:26 If ever you take your neighbor's cloak in pledge, you shall return it to him before the sun goes down,

22:27 For that is his only covering, and it is his cloak for his body — in what else shall he sleep? And if he cries to me, I will hear, for I am compassionate.

22:28 You shall not revile The Creator, nor curse a ruler of your people.

22:29 You shall not delay to offer from the fullness of your harvest and from the outflow of your presses. The firstborn of your sons you shall give to me.

22:31 You shall be consecrated to me. Therefore you shall not eat any flesh that is torn by beasts in the field — you shall throw it to the dogs.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExoda and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 22.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 22 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 22:21-24 — you shall not wrong a sojourner, for you were sojourners in the land of Egypt. You shall not mistreat any widow or fatherless child. If they cry to me, I will surely hear. The covenant laws embed the memory of Egypt directly into social justice legislation. The rationale for protecting the vulnerable is not abstract humanitarianism. It is covenant memory: you were slaves, you were sojourners, you were oppressed — therefore you may not do to others what was done to you. YHWH's hearing of the oppressed is repeated in this chapter as in Chapters 2 and 3. YHWH hears the cry of the vulnerable — and this hearing is the basis for social law.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 23 The Book of the Covenant Continued — The Three Feasts — The Angel

23:1 You shall not spread a false report. You shall not join hands with a wicked man to be a malicious witness.

23:2 You shall not fall in with the many to do evil, nor shall you bear witness in a lawsuit, siding with the many, so as to pervert justice.

23:4 If you meet your enemy's ox or his donkey going astray, you shall bring it back to him.

23:6 You shall not pervert the justice due to your poor in his lawsuit.

23:7 Keep far from a false charge, and do not kill the innocent and righteous, for I will not acquit the wicked.

23:8 And you shall take no bribe, for a bribe blinds the clear-sighted and subverts the cause of those who are in the right.

23:9 You shall not oppress a sojourner. You know the heart of a sojourner, for you were sojourners in the land of Egypt.

23:10 For six years you shall sow your land and gather in its yield,

23:11 But the seventh year you shall let it rest and lie fallow, that the poor of your people may eat. And what they leave the beasts of the field may eat. You shall do likewise with your vineyard, and with your olive orchard.

23:12 Six days you shall do your work, but on the seventh day you shall rest, that your ox and your donkey may have rest, and the son of your servant woman, and the sojourner, may be refreshed.

23:14 Three times in the year you shall keep a feast to me.

23:15 You shall keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread. As I commanded you, you shall eat unleavened bread for seven days at the appointed time in the month of Aviv, for in it you came out of Egypt. None shall appear before me empty-handed.

23:16 You shall keep the Feast of Harvest, of the firstfruits of your labor, of what you sow in the field. You shall keep the Feast of Ingathering at the end of the year, when you gather in from the field the fruit of your labor.

23:17 Three times in the year shall all your males appear before YHWH The Creator.

23:19 The best of the firstfruits of your ground you shall bring into the house of YHWH your Creator. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.

23:20 Behold, I send an angel before you to guard you on the way and to bring you to the place that I have prepared.

23:21 Pay careful attention to him and obey his voice. Do not rebel against him, for he will not pardon your transgression, for my name is in him.

23:22 But if you carefully obey his voice and do all that I say, then I will be an enemy to your enemies and an adversary to your adversaries.

23:27 I will send my terror before you and will throw into confusion all the people against whom you shall come, and I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you.

23:31 And I will set your border from the Red Sea to the Sea of the Philistines, and from the wilderness to the Euphrates, for I will give the inhabitants of the land into your hand, and you shall drive them out before you.

23:32 You shall make no covenant with them and their deities.

23:33 They shall not dwell in your land, lest they make you sin against me — for if you serve their deities, it will surely be a snare to you.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExoda and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 23. The three feast calendar and the angel sent before Yisra'EL are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 23 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 23:20-21 — I send an angel before you to guard you on the way. My name is in him. The angel sent before Yisra'EL carries YHWH's name within him. This is the Angel of Presence — the same figure referenced in Yovelim, in the Aqedah, at Peni'el, at the burning bush. The name being in the angel indicates that this messenger speaks and acts with full divine authority. When the angel speaks, YHWH speaks. The ancient Hebrew theology of the Angel of Presence preserved in the DSS and Orit Ge'ez treats this figure as YHWH's direct representative in whom the divine name dwells.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 24 The Covenant Ratified — Moshe Ascends the Mountain

24:1 Then he said to Moshe: Come up to YHWH, you and Aharon, Nadav, and Avihu, and seventy of the elders of Yisra'EL, and worship from afar.

24:3 Moshe came and told the people all the words of YHWH and all the rules. And all the people answered with one voice and said: All the words that YHWH has spoken we will do.

24:4 And Moshe wrote down all the words of YHWH. He rose early in the morning and built an altar at the foot of the mountain, and twelve pillars, according to the twelve tribes of Yisra'EL.

24:5 And he sent young men of the people of Yisra'EL, who offered burnt offerings and sacrificed peace offerings of oxen to YHWH.

24:6 And Moshe took half of the blood and put it in basins, and half of the blood he threw against the altar.

24:7 Then he took the Book of the Covenant and read it in the hearing of the people. And they said: All that YHWH has spoken we will do, and we will be obedient.

24:8 And Moshe took the blood and threw it on the people and said: Behold the blood of the covenant that YHWH has made with you in accordance with all these words.

24:9 Then Moshe and Aharon, Nadav, and Avihu, and seventy of the elders of Yisra'EL went up,

24:10 And they saw The Creator of Yisra'EL. There was under his feet as it were a pavement of sapphire stone, like the very heaven for clearness.

24:11 And he did not lay his hand on the chief men of the people of Yisra'EL — they beheld The Creator, and ate and drank.

24:12 YHWH said to Moshe: Come up to me on the mountain and wait there, that I may give you the tablets of stone, with the law and the commandment, which I have written for their instruction.

24:15 Then Moshe went up on the mountain, and the cloud covered the mountain.

24:16 The glory of YHWH dwelt on Mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it six days. And on the seventh day he called to Moshe out of the midst of the cloud.

24:17 Now the appearance of the glory of YHWH was like a devouring fire on the top of the mountain in the sight of the people of Yisra'EL.

24:18 Moshe entered the cloud and went up on the mountain. And Moshe was on the mountain forty days and forty nights.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExoda, 4QExodb, and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 24. The covenant ratification ceremony with the blood of the covenant — verse 24:8 — and the vision of the elders on the mountain — verses 24:9-11 — are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 24 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 24:9-11 — they saw The Creator of Yisra'EL. Under his feet was like sapphire stone, like heaven for clearness. They beheld The Creator and ate and drank. Seventy elders saw YHWH and ate a covenant meal in his presence on the mountain. The ancient record states this plainly. This is not a vision or a metaphor — the text says they saw, they beheld, they ate and drank. The Qumran community was attentive to these theophanic texts. The vision of YHWH's throne with the sapphire pavement connects directly to the throne visions of Yehezkel and to the throne room texts in the Qumran Shirot Olat HaShabbat — the Songs of the Sabbath Sacrifice — which describe the angelic worship around YHWH's throne in similar imagery.

THE MISHKAN INSTRUCTIONS — CHAPTERS 25 THROUGH 31

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 25 The Offering for the Mishkan — The Ark — The Menorah

25:1 YHWH said to Moshe,

25:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL, that they take for me a contribution. From every man whose heart moves him you shall receive the contribution for me.

25:3 And this is the contribution that you shall receive from them: gold, silver, and bronze,

25:4 Blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen, goats' hair,

25:5 Tanned rams' skins, goatskins, acacia wood,

25:6 Oil for the lamps, spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense,

25:7 Onyx stones, and stones for setting, for the ephod and for the breastpiece.

25:8 And let them make me a sanctuary, that I may dwell in their midst.

25:9 Exactly as I show you concerning the pattern of the tabernacle, and of all its furniture, so you shall make it.

25:10 They shall make an ark of acacia wood. Two cubits and a half shall be its length, a cubit and a half its breadth, and a cubit and a half its height.

25:11 You shall overlay it with pure gold, inside and outside shall you overlay it, and you shall make on it a molding of gold around it.

25:17 You shall make a mercy seat of pure gold. Two cubits and a half shall be its length, and a cubit and a half its breadth.

25:18 And you shall make two cheruvim of gold — of hammered work shall you make them — on the two ends of the mercy seat.

25:20 The cheruvim shall spread out their wings above, overshadowing the mercy seat with their wings, their faces one to another — toward the mercy seat shall the faces of the cheruvim be.

25:21 And you shall put the mercy seat on the top of the ark, and in the ark you shall put the testimony that I shall give you.

25:22 There I will meet with you, and from above the mercy seat, from between the two cheruvim that are on the ark of the testimony, I will speak with you about all that I will give you in commandment for the people of Yisra'EL.

25:23 You shall make a table of acacia wood. Two cubits shall be its length, a cubit its breadth, and a cubit and a half its height.

25:30 And you shall set the bread of the Presence on the table before me regularly.

25:31 You shall make a lampstand of pure gold. The lampstand shall be made of hammered work — its base, its stem, its cups, its calyxes, and its flowers shall be of one piece with it.

25:37 And you shall make its seven lamps, and the lamps shall be set up so as to give light on the space in front of it.

25:40 And see that you make them after the pattern for them, which is being shown you on the mountain.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExoda, 4QExodb, and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 25. The Ark and Menorah specifications are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 25 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QpaleoExodm presents a significantly expanded text in the Mishkan sections, including additional passages that parallel material in Chapters 35-40 where the Mishkan is constructed. The DSS version appears to have integrated the command and the fulfillment more closely than the Masoretic version does.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25:8 — let them make me a sanctuary, that I may dwell in their midst. The purpose of the Mishkan is stated in seven words: that I may dwell in their midst. The entire elaborate construction — the Ark, the mercy seat, the Menorah, the table, the curtains, the altar — serves a single purpose: to create a space where YHWH's presence can dwell among the people. The Mishkan is the portable Sinai. Sinai was where YHWH descended into fire and cloud. The Mishkan carries that point of presence through the wilderness.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 26 The Mishkan Structure — The Curtains — The Boards — The Veil

26:1 Moreover, you shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen and blue and purple and scarlet yarns — you shall make them with cheruvim skillfully worked into them.

26:6 And you shall make fifty clasps of gold, and couple the curtains one to the other with the clasps, so that the tabernacle may be a single whole.

26:7 You shall also make curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the tabernacle — eleven curtains shall you make.

26:14 And you shall make for the tent a covering of tanned rams' skins and a covering of goatskins on top.

26:15 You shall make upright frames for the tabernacle of acacia wood.

26:30 Then you shall erect the tabernacle according to the plan for it that you were shown on the mountain.

26:31 And you shall make a veil of blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen. It shall be made with cheruvim skillfully worked into it.

26:33 And you shall hang the veil from the clasps, and bring the ark of the testimony in there within the veil. And the veil shall separate for you the Holy Place from the Most Holy.

26:34 You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the testimony in the Most Holy Place.

26:35 And you shall set the table outside the veil, and the lampstand on the south side of the tabernacle opposite the table, and you shall put the table on the north side.

26:36 You shall make a screen for the entrance of the tent, of blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen, embroidered with needlework.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExoda and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 26.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 26 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 26:33 — the veil shall separate for you the Holy Place from the Most Holy. The Mishkan contains two chambers — the Holy Place and the Most Holy, the Devir, the inner sanctuary where the Ark rests. The Qumran community understood the two-chamber structure of the Mishkan as a template for the heavenly sanctuary. The Songs of the Sabbath Sacrifice from Cave 4 describe the heavenly sanctuary in multiple chambers with angelic priests serving in each. The earthly Mishkan is a copy of the heavenly pattern — stated explicitly in verse 25:40: make them after the pattern shown you on the mountain.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 27 The Altar of Burnt Offering — The Court — The Lamp Oil

27:1 You shall make the altar of acacia wood, five cubits long and five cubits broad. The altar shall be square, and its height shall be three cubits.

27:2 And you shall make horns for it on its four corners — its horns shall be of one piece with it — and you shall overlay it with bronze.

27:8 You shall make it hollow, with boards. As it has been shown you on the mountain, so shall it be made.

27:9 You shall make the court of the tabernacle. On the south side the court shall have hangings of fine twined linen a hundred cubits long for one side.

27:20 You shall command the people of Yisra'EL that they bring to you pure beaten olive oil for the light, that a lamp may regularly be set up to burn.

27:21 In the tent of meeting, outside the veil that is before the testimony, Aharon and his sons shall tend it from evening to morning before YHWH. It shall be a statute forever to be observed throughout their generations by the people of Yisra'EL.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExoda and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 27.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 27 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 27:20-21 — pure beaten olive oil for the light, that a lamp may regularly be set up to burn. The Menorah lamp burns continuously before the testimony — from evening to morning, tended by Aharon and his sons. The perpetual light before the Ark is the visible sign of YHWH's continuous presence. The lamp is never allowed to go out. This commandment is the origin of the Ner Tamid — the eternal light — that continued in the Temple and is maintained in every covenant community to this day as a witness to YHWH's uninterrupted presence.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 28 The Priestly Garments — The Ephod — The Breastpiece — The Twelve Stones

28:1 Then bring near to you Aharon your brother, and his sons with him, from among the people of Yisra'EL, to serve me as priests — Aharon and Aharon's sons Nadav, Avihu, El'azar, and Itamar.

28:2 And you shall make holy garments for Aharon your brother, for glory and for beauty.

28:4 These are the garments that they shall make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a coat of checker work, a turban, and a sash. They shall make holy garments for Aharon your brother and his sons to serve me as priests.

28:6 They shall make the ephod of gold, of blue and purple and scarlet yarns, and of fine twined linen, skillfully worked.

28:9 And you shall take two onyx stones, and engrave on them the names of the sons of Yisra'EL,

28:10 Six of their names on the one stone, and the names of the remaining six on the other stone, in the order of their birth.

28:12 And you shall set the two stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod, as stones of remembrance for the sons of Yisra'EL. And Aharon shall bear their names before YHWH on his two shoulders for remembrance.

28:15 You shall make a breastpiece of judgment, in skilled work. In the style of the ephod you shall make it — of gold, blue and purple and scarlet yarns, and fine twined linen shall you make it.

28:17 You shall set in it four rows of stones. A row of sardius, topaz, and carbuncle shall be the first row.

28:21 There shall be twelve stones with their names according to the names of the sons of Yisra'EL. They shall be like signets, each engraved with its name, for the twelve tribes.

28:29 So Aharon shall bear the names of the sons of Yisra'EL in the breastpiece of judgment on his heart, when he goes into the Holy Place, to bring them to regular remembrance before YHWH.

28:36 You shall make a plate of pure gold and engrave on it, like the engraving of a signet: Holy to YHWH.

28:38 It shall be on Aharon's forehead, and Aharon shall bear any guilt from the holy things that the people of Yisra'EL consecrate as their holy gifts. It shall regularly be on his forehead, that they may be accepted before YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExoda and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 28. The priestly garments are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 28 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 28:29 — Aharon shall bear the names of the sons of Yisra'EL in the breastpiece of judgment on his heart when he goes into the Holy Place, to bring them to regular remembrance before YHWH. The high priest enters the presence of YHWH carrying the names of all twelve tribes on his chest over his heart. Every act of priestly intercession in the Most Holy Place is a corporate act — the entire nation is carried in. No tribe is forgotten. No name is absent. The priestly ministry before YHWH is not individual — it is national and covenantal.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 29 The Consecration of the Priests — The Daily Offering

29:1 Now this is what you shall do to them to consecrate them, that they may serve me as priests. Take one bull of the herd and two rams without blemish,

29:4 And you shall bring Aharon and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting and wash them with water.

29:7 And you shall take the anointing oil and pour it on his head and anoint him.

29:18 And burn the whole ram on the altar. It is a burnt offering to YHWH. It is a pleasing aroma, a food offering to YHWH.

29:38 Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old day by day regularly.

29:39 One lamb you shall offer in the morning, and the other lamb you shall offer at twilight.

29:42 It shall be a regular burnt offering throughout your generations at the entrance of the tent of meeting before YHWH, where I will meet with you, to speak to you there.

29:43 There I will meet with the people of Yisra'EL, and it shall be sanctified by my glory.

29:45 I will dwell among the people of Yisra'EL and will be their Creator.

29:46 And they shall know that I am YHWH their Creator, who brought them out of the land of Egypt that I might dwell among them. I am YHWH their Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExoda and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 29.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 29 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 29:45-46 — I will dwell among the people of Yisra'EL and will be their Creator. And they shall know that I am YHWH their Creator, who brought them out of the land of Egypt that I might dwell among them. The purpose of the Exodus is stated again here at the culmination of the priestly consecration instructions. YHWH brought Yisra'EL out of Egypt not merely to give them a land or a law — but that he might dwell among them. The Mishkan is the fulfillment of the Exodus purpose. The journey from Egypt to Sinai to the Mishkan is the journey from slavery to the presence of YHWH.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 30 The Incense Altar — The Census Tax — The Anointing Oil — The Incense

30:1 You shall make an altar on which to burn incense — you shall make it of acacia wood.

30:6 And you shall put it in front of the veil that is above the ark of the testimony, in front of the mercy seat that is above the testimony, where I will meet with you.

30:7 And Aharon shall burn fragrant incense on it. Every morning when he dresses the lamps he shall burn it,

30:8 And when Aharon sets up the lamps at twilight, he shall burn it, a regular incense offering before YHWH throughout your generations.

30:12 When you take the census of the people of Yisra'EL, then each shall give a ransom for his life to YHWH when you number them, that there be no plague among them when you number them.

30:13 Each one who is numbered in the census shall give this: half a shekel according to the shekel of the sanctuary. The half shekel shall be a contribution to YHWH.

30:22 YHWH said to Moshe,

30:23 Take the finest spices: of liquid myrrh 500 shekels, and of sweet-smelling cinnamon half as much, that is, 250, and 250 of aromatic cane,

30:25 And you shall make of these a sacred anointing oil blended as by the perfumer — it shall be a holy anointing oil.

30:30 You shall anoint Aharon and his sons, and consecrate them, that they may serve me as priests.

30:34 YHWH said to Moshe: Take sweet spices, stacte, and onycha, and galbanum, sweet spices with pure frankincense — of each shall there be an equal part —

30:35 And make an incense blended as by the perfumer, seasoned with salt, pure and holy.

[DSS] 4QExoda and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 30.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 30:7-8 — Aharon shall burn fragrant incense every morning and at twilight. The rising incense before the mercy seat twice daily is the priestly representation of the prayers of the people ascending before YHWH. The incense altar stands directly in front of the veil — as close to the Most Holy Place as the regular priesthood could approach. The incense rising was understood in the ancient Hebrew tradition as prayer made visible. Tehillim 141:2 states this explicitly: let my prayer be counted as incense before you.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 31 The Craftsmen Betzal'el and Oholiav — The Sabbath as Covenant Sign — The Two Tablets

31:1 YHWH said to Moshe,

31:2 See, I have called by name Betzal'el the son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Yehudah,

31:3 And I have filled him with the Spirit of The Creator, with ability and intelligence, with knowledge and all craftsmanship,

31:4 To devise artistic designs, to work in gold, silver, and bronze,

31:5 In cutting stones for setting, and in carving wood, to work in every craft.

31:6 And behold, I have appointed with him Oholiav, the son of Ahisamakh, of the tribe of Dan. And I have given to all able men ability, that they may make all that I have commanded you.

31:12 And YHWH said to Moshe,

31:13 You are to speak to the people of Yisra'EL and say: Above all you shall keep my Sabbaths, for this is a sign between me and you throughout your generations, that you may know that I, YHWH, sanctify you.

31:14 You shall keep the Sabbath, because it is holy for you. Everyone who profanes it shall be put to death. Whoever does any work on it, that soul shall be cut off from among his people.

31:15 Six days shall work be done, but the seventh day is a Sabbath of solemn rest, holy to YHWH. Whoever does any work on the Sabbath day shall be put to death.

31:16 Therefore the people of Yisra'EL shall keep the Sabbath, observing the Sabbath throughout their generations, as a covenant forever.

31:17 It is a sign forever between me and the people of Yisra'EL that in six days YHWH made heaven and earth, and on the seventh day he rested and was refreshed.

31:18 And he gave to Moshe, when he had finished speaking with him on Mount Sinai, the two tablets of the testimony, tablets of stone, written with the finger of The Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExoda and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 31. The Sabbath as covenant sign and the tablets written by the finger of The Creator are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 31 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 31:13 — the Sabbath is a sign between me and you throughout your generations, that you may know that I, YHWH, sanctify you. The Sabbath is the covenant sign — the external marker of the relationship between YHWH and Yisra'EL. Circumcision is the covenant sign in the flesh given to Avraham. The Sabbath is the covenant sign in time given at Sinai. The two signs together — one in the body, one in the week — constitute the full covenant identity of the ancient Hebrew community as preserved in the DSS and the Orit Ge'ez.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 31:3 — I have filled him with the Spirit of The Creator, with ability and intelligence, with knowledge and all craftsmanship. Betzal'el is the first person in the Torah explicitly said to be filled with the Spirit of The Creator for a specific task. That task is artistic craftsmanship. The Spirit of The Creator produces beauty. The making of the Mishkan is a Spirit-filled act — not merely technical construction but sacred artistry under divine anointing.

THE GOLDEN CALF — THE RENEWAL — THE MISHKAN BUILT — CHAPTERS 32 THROUGH 40

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 32 The Golden Calf — Moshe's Intercession — The Levites — The Plague

32:1 When the people saw that Moshe delayed to come down from the mountain, the people gathered themselves together to Aharon and said to him: Up, make us deities who shall go before us. As for this Moshe, the man who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.

32:2 So Aharon said to them: Take off the rings of gold that are in the ears of your wives, your sons, and your daughters, and bring them to me.

32:3 So all the people took off the rings of gold that were in their ears and brought them to Aharon.

32:4 And he received the gold from their hand and fashioned it with a graving tool and made a golden calf. And they said: These are your deities, O Yisra'EL, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt.

32:5 When Aharon saw this, he built an altar before it. And Aharon made a proclamation and said: Tomorrow shall be a feast to YHWH.

32:6 And they rose up early the next day and offered burnt offerings and brought peace offerings. And the people sat down to eat and drink and rose up to play.

32:7 And YHWH said to Moshe: Go down, for your people, whom you brought up out of the land of Egypt, have corrupted themselves.

32:8 They have turned aside quickly out of the way that I commanded them. They have made for themselves a golden calf and have worshiped it and sacrificed to it and said: These are your deities, O Yisra'EL, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt.

32:9 And YHWH said to Moshe: I have seen this people, and behold, it is a stiff-necked people.

32:10 Now therefore let me alone, that my wrath may burn hot against them and I may consume them, in order that I may make a great nation of you.

32:11 But Moshe implored YHWH his Creator and said: O YHWH, why does your wrath burn hot against your people, whom you have brought out of the land of Egypt with great power and with a mighty hand?

32:12 Why should the Egyptians say: With evil intent did he bring them out, to kill them in the mountains and to consume them from the face of the earth? Turn from your burning anger and relent from this disaster against your people.

32:13 Remember Avraham, Yitzhak, and Yisra'EL, your servants, to whom you swore by your own self, and said to them: I will multiply your offspring as the stars of heaven, and all this land that I have promised I will give to your offspring, and they shall inherit it forever.

32:14 And YHWH relented from the disaster that he had spoken of bringing on his people.

32:15 Then Moshe turned and went down from the mountain with the two tablets of the testimony in his hand, tablets that were written on both sides — on the front and on the back they were written.

32:16 The tablets were the work of The Creator, and the writing was the writing of The Creator, engraved on the tablets.

32:19 And as soon as he came near the camp and saw the calf and the dancing, Moshe's anger burned hot, and he threw the tablets out of his hands and broke them at the foot of the mountain.

32:20 He took the calf that they had made and burned it with fire and ground it to powder and scattered it on the water and made the people of Yisra'EL drink it.

32:25 And when Moshe saw that the people had broken loose — for Aharon had let them break loose, to the derision of their enemies —

32:26 Then Moshe stood in the gate of the camp and said: Who is on YHWH's side? Come to me. And all the sons of Levi gathered around him.

32:27 And he said to them: Thus says YHWH The Creator of Yisra'EL — Put your sword on your side each of you, and go to and fro from gate to gate throughout the camp, and each of you kill his brother and his companion and his neighbor.

32:28 And the sons of Levi did according to the word of Moshe. And that day about three thousand men of the people fell.

32:30 The next day Moshe said to the people: You have sinned a great sin. And now I will go up to YHWH — perhaps I can make atonement for your sin.

32:31 So Moshe returned to YHWH and said: Alas, this people has sinned a great sin. They have made for themselves deities of gold.

32:32 But now, if you will forgive their sin — but if not, please blot me out of your book that you have written.

32:33 But YHWH said to Moshe: Whoever has sinned against me, I will blot out of my book.

32:34 But now go, lead the people to the place about which I have spoken to you. Behold, my angel shall go before you. Nevertheless, in the day when I visit, I will visit their sin upon them.

32:35 Then YHWH sent a plague on the people, because they made the calf, the one that Aharon made.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExodc and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 32. The golden calf account is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts. 4QpaleoExodm presents a notably expanded text at several points in this chapter, consistent with its pattern throughout Shemot.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 32 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 32:13 — remember Avraham, Yitzhak, and Yisra'EL, your servants, to whom you swore by your own self. Moshe's intercession at the golden calf is built entirely on the Avrahamic covenant. He does not argue that the people deserve mercy. He argues that YHWH swore by himself — the irrevocable oath of Bereshit 22:16. Moshe uses YHWH's own covenant commitment as the ground of his appeal. The intercession works. Verse 32:14: YHWH relented. The covenant with Avraham is more durable than the sin at Sinai.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 32:32 — if you will not forgive their sin, please blot me out of your book. Moshe offers himself in place of the people. This is the covenant intercessor at his most extreme — offering his own covenant standing for the survival of the people he leads. YHWH does not accept the trade but does not dismiss the offer. The offer itself demonstrates the character required to carry the covenant forward.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 33 YHWH's Presence — The Tent of Meeting — Moshe's Face — The Request to See Glory

33:1 YHWH said to Moshe: Depart — go up from here, you and the people whom you have brought up out of the land of Egypt, to the land of which I swore to Avraham, Yitzhak, and Yaakov, saying: To your offspring I will give it.

33:3 Go up to a land flowing with milk and honey — but I will not go up among you, lest I consume you on the way, for you are a stiff-necked people.

33:4 When the people heard this disastrous word, they mourned, and no one put on his ornaments.

33:7 Now Moshe used to take the tent and pitch it outside the camp, far off from the camp, and he called it the tent of meeting. And everyone who sought YHWH would go out to the tent of meeting, which was outside the camp.

33:8 Whenever Moshe went out to the tent, all the people would rise up, and each would stand at his tent door, and watch until Moshe had gone into the tent.

33:9 When Moshe entered the tent, the pillar of cloud would descend and stand at the entrance of the tent, and YHWH would speak with Moshe.

33:10 And when all the people saw the pillar of cloud standing at the entrance of the tent, all the people would rise up and worship, each at his tent door.

33:11 Thus YHWH used to speak to Moshe face to face, as a man speaks to his friend. When Moshe turned again into the camp, his assistant Yehoshua the son of Nun, a young man, would not depart from the tent.

33:12 Moshe said to YHWH: See, you say to me: Bring up this people — but you have not let me know whom you will send with me. Yet you have said: I know you by name, and you have also found favor in my sight.

33:13 Now therefore, if I have found favor in your sight, please show me now your ways, that I may know you in order to find favor in your sight. Consider too that this nation is your people.

33:14 And he said: My presence will go with you, and I will give you rest.

33:15 And he said to him: If your presence will not go with me, do not bring us up from here.

33:16 For how shall it be known that I have found favor in your sight, I and your people? Is it not in your going with us, so that we are distinct, I and your people, from every other people on the face of the earth?

33:17 And YHWH said to Moshe: This very thing that you have spoken I will do, for you have found favor in my sight, and I know you by name.

33:18 Moshe said: Please show me your glory.

33:19 And he said: I will make all my goodness pass before you and will proclaim before you my name YHWH. And I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and will show mercy on whom I will show mercy.

33:20 But he said: You cannot see my face, for man shall not see me and live.

33:21 And YHWH said: Behold, there is a place by me where you shall stand on the rock,

33:22 And while my glory passes by I will put you in a cleft of the rock, and I will cover you with my hand until I have passed by.

33:23 Then I will take away my hand, and you shall see my back — but my face shall not be seen.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExodd and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 33. The dialogue between Moshe and YHWH regarding the divine presence is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 33 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 33:11 — YHWH used to speak to Moshe face to face, as a man speaks to his friend. And verse 33:20 — you cannot see my face, for man shall not see me and live. The apparent contradiction is the ancient record's way of describing two different encounters. The first — face to face, as a friend speaks — describes the intimacy and directness of the prophetic communication, not literal visual confrontation. The second — you cannot see my face — describes the full unmediated manifestation of YHWH's glory. Moshe experiences both: intimacy without the full glory. This is the unique standing of Moshe in the prophetic tradition — closer than any other, yet still protected by the cleft of the rock.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 34 The New Tablets — The Thirteen Attributes — The Covenant Renewed

34:1 YHWH said to Moshe: Cut for yourself two tablets of stone like the first, and I will write on the tablets the words that were on the first tablets, which you broke.

34:2 Be ready by the morning, and come up in the morning to Mount Sinai, and present yourself there to me on the top of the mountain.

34:4 So Moshe cut two tablets of stone like the first. And he rose early in the morning and went up on Mount Sinai, as YHWH had commanded him, and took in his hand two tablets of stone.

34:5 YHWH descended in the cloud and stood with him there, and proclaimed the name of YHWH.

34:6 YHWH passed before him and proclaimed: YHWH, YHWH, a Creator merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness,

34:7 Keeping steadfast love for thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin, but who will by no means clear the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children and the children's children, to the third and the fourth generation.

34:8 And Moshe quickly bowed his head toward the earth and worshiped.

34:9 And he said: If now I have found favor in your sight, O YHWH, please let YHWH go in the midst of us, for it is a stiff-necked people, and pardon our iniquity and our sin, and take us for your inheritance.

34:10 And he said: Behold, I am making a covenant. Before all your people I will do marvels, such as have not been created in all the earth or in any nation. And all the people among whom you are shall see the work of YHWH, for it is an awesome thing that I will do with you.

34:14 For you shall worship no other deity, for YHWH, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous Creator —

34:18 You shall keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the time appointed in the month Aviv, for in the month Aviv you came out from Egypt.

34:21 Six days you shall work, but on the seventh day you shall rest. In plowing time and in harvest you shall rest.

34:22 You shall observe the Feast of Weeks, the firstfruits of wheat harvest, and the Feast of Ingathering at the year's end.

34:27 And YHWH said to Moshe: Write these words, for in accordance with these words I have made a covenant with you and with Yisra'EL.

34:28 So he was there with YHWH forty days and forty nights. He neither ate bread nor drank water. And he wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant, the Ten Words.

34:29 When Moshe came down from Mount Sinai, with the two tablets of the testimony in his hand as he came down from the mountain, Moshe did not know that the skin of his face shone because he had been talking with The Creator.

34:30 Aharon and all the people of Yisra'EL saw Moshe, and behold, the skin of his face shone, and they were afraid to come near him.

34:33 And when Moshe had finished speaking with them, he put a veil over his face.

34:34 But whenever Moshe went in before YHWH to speak with him, he would remove the veil, until he came out. And when he came out and told the people of Yisra'EL what he was commanded,

34:35 The people of Yisra'EL would see the face of Moshe, that the skin of Moshe's face was shining. And Moshe would put the veil over his face again, until he went in to speak with him.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExodd and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 34. The Thirteen Attributes of YHWH proclaimed in verses 34:6-7 are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts and appear also in multiple DSS liturgical texts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 34 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 34:6-7 — YHWH, YHWH, a Creator merciful and gracious, slow to anger, abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness, keeping steadfast love for thousands, forgiving iniquity and transgression and sin. These are the Thirteen Attributes — the self-declaration YHWH makes of his own character at the renewal of the covenant after the golden calf. This is YHWH naming himself by his character, not by his power. The first thing YHWH declares about himself after the greatest covenant failure is mercy, grace, patience, steadfast love, faithfulness, forgiveness. The ancient Hebrew theology of the divine character begins here.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 34:29 — the skin of Moshe's face shone because he had been talking with The Creator. The radiance of Moshe's face after forty days in YHWH's presence is the physical evidence of the encounter. The ancient record states that Moshe did not know his face was shining. The light was not a performance. It was the natural result of sustained proximity to YHWH's glory. The ancient text does not explain the mechanism — it simply states the fact and its effect on the people.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 35 The Sabbath Restated — The Freewill Offering for the Mishkan

35:1 Moshe assembled all the congregation of the people of Yisra'EL and said to them: These are the things that YHWH has commanded you to do.

35:2 Six days work shall be done, but on the seventh day you shall have a Sabbath of solemn rest, holy to YHWH. Whoever does any work on it shall be put to death.

35:3 You shall kindle no fire in all your dwelling places on the Sabbath day.

35:4 Moshe said to all the congregation of the people of Yisra'EL: This is the thing that YHWH has commanded.

35:5 Take from among you a contribution to YHWH. Whoever is of a generous heart, let him bring YHWH's contribution — gold, silver, and bronze.

35:21 And they came, everyone whose heart stirred him, and everyone whose spirit moved him, and brought YHWH's contribution to be used for the tent of meeting, and for all its service, and for the holy garments.

35:22 So they came, both men and women. All who were of a willing heart brought brooches and earrings and signet rings and armlets, all sorts of gold objects, every man dedicating an offering of gold to YHWH.

35:25 And every skillful woman spun with her hands, and they all brought what they had spun in blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen.

35:29 All the men and women, the people of Yisra'EL, whose heart moved them to bring anything for the work that YHWH had commanded by Moshe to be done brought it as a freewill offering to YHWH.

35:30 Then Moshe said to the people of Yisra'EL: See, YHWH has called by name Betzal'el the son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Yehudah.

35:31 And he has filled him with the Spirit of The Creator, with skill, with intelligence, with knowledge, and with all craftsmanship,

35:34 And he has inspired him to teach, both him and Oholiav the son of Ahisamakh of the tribe of Dan.

35:35 He has filled them with skill to do every sort of work done by an engraver or by a designer or by an embroiderer in blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen, or by a weaver — by any sort of workman or skilled designer.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExode and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 35.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 35 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 35:21 — everyone whose heart stirred him and whose spirit moved him. The construction of the Mishkan is funded by voluntary offering — not taxation, not conscription. The entire material provision for the dwelling of YHWH's presence among the people comes from those whose hearts and spirits are moved. The ancient record emphasizes this twice — verse 35:21 and verse 35:29. The Mishkan is built on willing generosity, not compulsion.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 36 The Craftsmen Begin — The Freewill Offering Exceeds the Need

36:1 Betzal'el and Oholiav and every craftsman in whom YHWH has put skill and intelligence to know how to do any work in the construction of the sanctuary shall work in accordance with all that YHWH has commanded.

36:3 And they received from Moshe all the contribution that the people of Yisra'EL had brought for doing the work on the sanctuary. They still kept bringing him freewill offerings every morning,

36:4 So that all the craftsmen who were doing every sort of task on the sanctuary came, each from the task that he was doing,

36:5 And said to Moshe: The people bring much more than enough for doing the work that YHWH has commanded us to do.

36:6 So Moshe gave command, and word was proclaimed throughout the camp: Let no man or woman do anything more for the contribution for the sanctuary. So the people were restrained from bringing,

36:7 For the material they had was sufficient to do all the work, and more.

36:8 And all the craftsmen among the workmen made the Mishkan with ten curtains. They were made of fine twined linen and blue and purple and scarlet yarns, with cherubim skillfully worked.

36:14 He also made curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the Mishkan — eleven curtains in all.

36:20 Then he made the upright frames for the Mishkan of acacia wood.

36:35 He made the veil of blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen — with cherubim skillfully worked into it he made it.

36:37 He made a screen for the entrance of the tent, of blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen, embroidered with needlework.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExode and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 36.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 36 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 36:5-7 — the people bring much more than enough. Moshe has to command the people to stop bringing offerings. The generosity of the people for the Mishkan overflows the need. This is the contrast the ancient record presents against the golden calf: the same people who gave their gold earrings for an idol in Chapter 32 now give so abundantly for the Mishkan that the craftsmen cannot use it all. The covenant renewal of Chapters 33-34 has changed what the gold is used for.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 37 The Ark Made — The Table — The Menorah — The Incense Altar

37:1 Betzal'el made the ark of acacia wood. Two cubits and a half was its length, a cubit and a half its breadth, and a cubit and a half its height.

37:2 And he overlaid it with pure gold inside and outside, and made a molding of gold around it.

37:3 And he cast for it four rings of gold for its four feet — two rings on its one side and two rings on its other side.

37:6 And he made a mercy seat of pure gold. Two cubits and a half was its length, and a cubit and a half its breadth.

37:7 And he made two cherubim of gold. He made them of hammered work on the two ends of the mercy seat,

37:9 The cherubim spread out their wings above, overshadowing the mercy seat with their wings, with their faces one to another — toward the mercy seat were the faces of the cherubim.

37:10 He also made the table of acacia wood. Two cubits was its length, a cubit its breadth, and a cubit and a half its height.

37:17 He also made the lampstand of pure gold. He made the lampstand of hammered work. Its base, its stem, its cups, its calyxes, and its flowers were of one piece with it.

37:25 He made the altar of incense of acacia wood. Its length was a cubit, and its breadth was a cubit. It was square, and two cubits was its height. Its horns were of one piece with it.

37:26 And he overlaid it with pure gold, its top and around its sides and its horns. And he made a molding of gold around it.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExode and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 37.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 37 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 37:9 — the cherubim spread out their wings above, overshadowing the mercy seat with their faces one to another — toward the mercy seat. The cherubim face the mercy seat — the kapporet — which is the place of atonement, the place where YHWH declared he would meet with Moshe. They do not face outward. They face the place of meeting. The Ark is oriented entirely toward YHWH's presence. The cherubim are described in the same posture throughout Yehezkel's vision and in the heavenly sanctuary described in Chanokh. The Mishkan's design mirrors the heavenly original.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 38 The Altar of Burnt Offering — The Laver — The Court — The Account of Materials

38:1 He made the altar of burnt offering of acacia wood. Five cubits was its length, and five cubits its breadth. It was square, and three cubits was its height.

38:2 And he made horns for it on its four corners. Its horns were of one piece with it, and he overlaid it with bronze.

38:8 He made the basin of bronze and its stand of bronze, from the mirrors of the ministering women who ministered in the entrance of the tent of meeting.

38:9 And he made the court. For the south side the hangings of the court were of fine twined linen, a hundred cubits.

38:21 These are the records of the Mishkan, the Mishkan of the testimony, as they were recorded at the commandment of Moshe, the work of the Levites under the direction of Itamar the son of Aharon the priest.

38:22 Betzal'el the son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Yehudah, made all that YHWH commanded Moshe.

38:23 And with him was Oholiav the son of Ahisamakh, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver and designer and embroiderer in blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen.

38:24 All the gold that was used for the work, in all the construction of the sanctuary, the gold from the offering, was twenty-nine talents and 730 shekels, by the shekel of the sanctuary.

38:25 The silver from those of the congregation who were listed was a hundred talents and 1,775 shekels, by the shekel of the sanctuary.

38:27 The hundred talents of silver were for casting the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the veil — a hundred bases for the hundred talents, a talent a base.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExode and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 38.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 38 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 38:8 — the basin of bronze made from the mirrors of the ministering women. The laver — the basin for ritual washing — is made from the bronze mirrors of women who ministered at the entrance of the tent of meeting. The ancient record records the existence of women who ministered at the sanctuary entrance before the Mishkan is even completed. Their mirrors — instruments of personal adornment — are donated and transformed into the instrument of priestly purification. The ancient text names this origin specifically. It is not incidental.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 39 The Priestly Garments Made — All the Work Completed

39:1 From the blue and purple and scarlet yarns they made finely woven garments, for ministering in the holy place. They made the holy garments for Aharon, as YHWH had commanded Moshe.

39:2 He made the ephod of gold, blue and purple and scarlet yarns, and fine twined linen.

39:8 He made the breastpiece, in skilled work, in the style of the ephod, of gold, blue and purple and scarlet yarns, and fine twined linen.

39:10 And they set in it four rows of stones. A row of sardius, topaz, and carbuncle was the first row.

39:11 And the second row, an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond.

39:12 And the third row, a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst.

39:13 And the fourth row, a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper. They were enclosed in settings of gold filigree.

39:14 There were twelve stones with their names according to the names of the sons of Yisra'EL. They were like signets, each engraved with its name, for the twelve tribes.

39:22 He also made the robe of the ephod woven all of blue,

39:25 They also made bells of pure gold, and put the bells between the pomegranates all around the hem of the robe, between the pomegranates —

39:26 A bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate around the hem of the robe for ministering, as YHWH had commanded Moshe.

39:30 They made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold, and wrote on it an inscription, like the engraving of a signet: Holy to YHWH.

39:32 Thus all the work of the Mishkan of the tent of meeting was finished, and the people of Yisra'EL did according to all that YHWH had commanded Moshe — so they did.

39:33 And they brought the Mishkan to Moshe, the tent and all its utensils, its hooks, its frames, its bars, its pillars, and its bases.

39:42 According to all that YHWH had commanded Moshe, so the people of Yisra'EL had done all the work.

39:43 And Moshe saw all the work, and behold, they had done it — as YHWH had commanded, so had they done it. Then Moshe blessed them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExode and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 39.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 39 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 39:43 — Moshe saw all the work, and behold, they had done it as YHWH had commanded. Then Moshe blessed them. The completion of the Mishkan echoes the completion of creation in Bereshit 1-2. At the end of creation YHWH saw all that he had made and declared it good. At the end of the Mishkan construction Moshe saw all the work and blessed the people. The deliberate structural parallel between the creation account and the Mishkan construction is one of the most significant literary correspondences in the entire Torah. The Mishkan is the creation of a new sacred space — a micro-cosmos where heaven and earth meet.

SHEMOT — CHAPTER 40 The Mishkan Erected — The Glory of YHWH Fills It — The Cloud and Fire

40:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

40:2 On the first day of the first month you shall erect the Mishkan of the tent of meeting.

40:3 And you shall put in it the ark of the testimony, and you shall screen the ark with the veil.

40:4 And you shall bring in the table and arrange it, and you shall bring in the lampstand and set up its lamps.

40:5 And you shall put the golden altar for incense before the ark of the testimony, and set up the screen for the door of the Mishkan.

40:6 You shall set the altar of burnt offering before the door of the Mishkan of the tent of meeting,

40:7 And place the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar, and put water in it.

40:9 You shall take the anointing oil and anoint the Mishkan and all that is in it, and consecrate it and all its furniture, so that it may become holy.

40:12 Then you shall bring Aharon and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting and shall wash them with water

40:13 And put on Aharon the holy garments. And you shall anoint him and consecrate him, that he may serve me as priest.

40:16 This Moshe did — according to all that YHWH commanded him, so he did.

40:17 In the first month in the second year, on the first day of the month, the Mishkan was erected.

40:20 He took the testimony and put it into the ark, and put the poles on the ark and set the mercy seat above on the ark.

40:21 And he brought the ark into the Mishkan and set up the veil of the screen, and screened the ark of the testimony, as YHWH had commanded Moshe.

40:24 He put the lampstand in the tent of meeting, opposite the table on the south side of the Mishkan,

40:26 And he put the golden altar in the tent of meeting before the veil,

40:29 And he set the altar of burnt offering at the entrance of the Mishkan of the tent of meeting, and offered on it the burnt offering and the grain offering, as YHWH had commanded Moshe.

40:30 He set the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar, and put water in it for washing,

40:32 When they went into the tent of meeting, and when they approached the altar, they washed, as YHWH commanded Moshe.

40:33 And he erected the court around the Mishkan and the altar, and set up the screen of the gate of the court. So Moshe finished the work.

40:34 Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of YHWH filled the Mishkan.

40:35 And Moshe was not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud settled on it, and the glory of YHWH filled the Mishkan.

40:36 Throughout all their journeys, whenever the cloud was taken up from over the Mishkan, the people of Yisra'EL would set out.

40:37 But if the cloud was not taken up, then they did not set out till the day that it was taken up.

40:38 For the cloud of YHWH was on the Mishkan by day, and fire was in it by night, in the sight of all the house of Yisra'EL throughout all their journeys.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QExodf and 4QpaleoExodm preserve portions of Chapter 40. The erection of the Mishkan and the descent of YHWH's glory are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts. This is the climax of the most extensively preserved book in the Qumran Exodus collection.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 40 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 40:34-35 — the cloud covered the tent of meeting, and the glory of YHWH filled the Mishkan. And Moshe was not able to enter because the cloud settled on it and the glory of YHWH filled it. The glory of YHWH — the Kavod YHWH — descends and fills the Mishkan at the moment of its completion. The result is that even Moshe, the one who spoke with YHWH face to face as a friend, cannot enter. The fullness of YHWH's manifest presence overwhelms even the greatest prophet. This is the culmination of everything in Shemot: YHWH, who declared I will go down with you in Bereshit 46:4, has now established his dwelling in the midst of the people. The Exodus ends not with arrival in the land but with the descent of YHWH's glory into a tent in the wilderness.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 40:38 — the cloud of YHWH was on the Mishkan by day, and fire was in it by night, in the sight of all the house of Yisra'EL throughout all their journeys. The pillar of cloud and fire that led them out of Egypt in Chapter 13 now rests on the Mishkan permanently. YHWH does not lead from a distance. He dwells in the center of the camp. The presence that separated Egypt from Yisra'EL at the sea now marks the geographic and theological center of the covenant people's life in the wilderness.
